questionHtml:
How many dichlorinated butane isomers can be formed by the halogenation of CH3CH2CH2CH3 with excess Cl2 in the presence of UV light?

A.  4

B.  6

C.  8

D.  10

meta.title: How many dichlorinated butane isomers can be formed by the halogenation of CH3CH2CH2CH3 with excess Cl2 in the presence of UV light? A. 4 B. 6 C. 8 D. 10
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 3. What are the mechanisms of chemical change?

Reactivity 3.3.3—Radicals take part in substitution reactions with alkanes, producing a mixture of products. Explain, using equations, the propagation and termination steps in the reactions between alkanes and halogens.
